---
title: "Joint convexity of quantum relative entropy: literature context"
subtitle: "References and proof-route comparison for `qRelativeEnt_joint_convexity`"
format: html
---

This note places the formalized theorem and its proof route in the published literature,
and records exactly which citations were verified while preparing this documentation
(see §5). The result itself is classical; the route used in the Lean proof is assembled
from standard published ingredients, as detailed below.

## 1. Joint convexity of the Umegaki relative entropy

The Umegaki relative entropy $D(\rho\|\sigma) = \operatorname{Tr}[\rho(\log\rho -
\log\sigma)]$ was introduced by Umegaki [Umegaki62]. Its joint convexity,
$$
D\Bigl(\textstyle\sum_i p_i \rho_i \,\Big\|\, \sum_i p_i \sigma_i\Bigr)
\le \sum_i p_i\, D(\rho_i\|\sigma_i),
$$
is due to Lindblad [Lindblad74], as a consequence of Lieb's concavity theorem
[Lieb73] (joint concavity of $(A,B) \mapsto \operatorname{Tr}[K^\dagger A^s K
B^{1-s}]$, proved by Lieb to settle the Wigner–Yanase–Dyson conjecture). It is
essentially equivalent to the strong subadditivity (SSA) of von Neumann entropy proved
by Lieb and Ruskai [LiebRuskai73; LiebRuskaiPRL73], and it implies — via Lindblad
[Lindblad75] and Uhlmann [Uhlmann77] — monotonicity of $D$ under completely positive
trace-preserving maps (the data-processing inequality). Later, structurally different
proofs of joint convexity were given, e.g. via quasi-entropies and operator convexity
(Petz [Petz86]), via operator perspective functions (Effros [Effros09]), and Tropp
[Tropp12] showed the implication can be reversed (joint convexity $\Rightarrow$ Lieb's
theorem). A self-contained modern exposition of the classical route is Carlen's lecture
notes [Carlen10].

## 2. The sandwiched Rényi relative entropy

The sandwiched Rényi relative entropy
$$
\widetilde D_\alpha(\rho\|\sigma) = \frac{1}{\alpha-1} \log \operatorname{Tr}\!\Bigl[
\bigl(\sigma^{\frac{1-\alpha}{2\alpha}}\, \rho\, \sigma^{\frac{1-\alpha}{2\alpha}}
\bigr)^{\alpha}\Bigr]
$$
was introduced independently by Müller-Lennert, Dupuis, Szehr, Fehr and Tomamichel
[MLDSFT13] and by Wilde, Winter and Yang [WWY14]. Its key properties, as relevant
here:

- **$\alpha \to 1$ limit.** $\widetilde D_\alpha \to D$ (Umegaki) as $\alpha \to 1$;
  established in [MLDSFT13] (see also [WWY14]). This is the fact formalized as
  continuity of $\alpha \mapsto \widetilde D_\alpha$ at $\alpha = 1$
  (`sandwichedRelRentropy.continuousOn`).
- **Data processing.** Monotonicity under CPTP maps holds for all $\alpha \ge 1/2$.
  [MLDSFT13] proved it for a partial range of $\alpha$ and conjectured the general
  case; it was then proved for $\alpha > 1$ by Beigi [Beigi13] via complex
  interpolation, and by Frank and Lieb [FrankLieb13] for the full range
  $\alpha \ge 1/2$ via a variational argument. Leditzky, Rouzé and Datta
  [LRD17] revisit the Frank–Lieb variational proof to characterize equality; the Lean
  development of the sandwiched DPI (and of the joint convexity of the trace functional
  $\widetilde Q_\alpha$) follows this Frank–Lieb/Leditzky–Rouzé–Datta presentation:
  variational formula $\widetilde Q_\alpha = \sup_{H \ge 0} f_\alpha(H,\rho,\sigma)$,
  joint convexity of each $f_\alpha(H,\cdot,\cdot)$, twirling, and Stinespring dilation.
- **Operational meaning** for $\alpha > 1$ (strong converse exponents): Mosonyi and
  Ogawa [MosonyiOgawa15]; textbook treatments in Tomamichel [Tomamichel16] and Wilde
  [Wilde17].

At the matrix-analysis level, the convexity input behind the $\alpha > 1$ joint
convexity of $\widetilde Q_\alpha$ is the concavity of
$\sigma \mapsto \operatorname{Tr}[(\sigma^{s} H \sigma^{s})^{q}]$ with
$s = \frac{\alpha-1}{2\alpha}$, $q = \frac{\alpha}{\alpha-1}$ (note $sq = 1/2$, i.e. the
functional is homogeneous of degree one in $\sigma$ — the borderline case). Concavity
statements of this type belong to the Epstein/Hiai family [Epstein73; Hiai13] and are
the engine of the Frank–Lieb argument. In the Lean library this concavity
(`HermitianMat.trace_conj_rpow_concave`) is derived from the Lieb and Ando trace
theorems formalized in `QuantumInfo/ForMathlib/HayataGroup/TraceInequality/`
[Lieb73; Ando79].

## 3. Comparison of proof routes

**The classical route** (Lindblad [Lindblad74], exposition in [Carlen10]): take
Lieb's jointly concave functional $(A,B) \mapsto \operatorname{Tr}[K^\dagger A^s K
B^{1-s}]$ and obtain joint convexity of $D$ by a limit of difference quotients at the
degenerate parameter value ($s \to 1$), where the functional reduces to a trace and its
derivative produces the logarithms. This is the route taken, in one form or another, by
most textbooks; the modern short proofs [Effros09; Tropp12] replace the limit by
perspective-function arguments.

**The route formalized here**: take the *sandwiched* trace functional
$\widetilde Q_\alpha$, jointly convex for $\alpha > 1$ by the Frank–Lieb variational
argument [FrankLieb13; LRD17], and pass to the limit $\alpha \to 1^{+}$, where
$\widetilde D_\alpha \to D$ [MLDSFT13]. The two elementary bounds $\log x \le x - 1$
and $e^x \le 1 + x + x^2$ (for $|x| \le 1$) convert convexity of $\widetilde Q_\alpha$
into an eventual convex-combination bound on $\widetilde D_\alpha$ with a majorant that
converges by continuity — so no differentiability at $\alpha = 1$ is needed.

Three honest observations about this comparison:

1. **The architecture is the same.** Both routes run: *jointly convex/concave
   one-parameter trace functional* $\to$ *limit at the degenerate parameter value where
   the relative entropy emerges as a derivative*. The formalized proof is the sandwiched
   analogue of Lindblad's argument, with the Wigner–Yanase–Dyson-type functional
   replaced by $\widetilde Q_\alpha$, and with a one-sided convergent majorant standing
   in for the derivative.
2. **The route is assembled from standard published pieces, but is not the textbook
   route to *this* theorem.** Joint convexity of the Umegaki entropy predates the
   sandwiched family by four decades, so the published literature invariably proves it
   by the classical route of §1. The $\alpha \to 1^{+}$ mechanism used here appears in
   the literature as the standard way to transfer sandwiched-Rényi facts to the Umegaki
   entropy — e.g. the Umegaki DPI as the $\alpha \to 1$ limit of the sandwiched DPI
   [MLDSFT13; Wilde17], which is exactly how the library already proves
   `sandwichedRenyiEntropy_DPI_eq_one`. Applying the same limiting pattern to joint
   convexity is mathematically routine and, we believe, folklore; we do not claim a
   specific published reference whose stated result is "joint convexity of Umegaki via
   sandwiched $\alpha \to 1^{+}$", and we are not aware of one.
3. **Both routes rest on the same analytic core.** Whether one goes through
   $\operatorname{Tr}[K^\dagger A^s K B^{1-s}]$ or through $\widetilde Q_\alpha$, the
   deep input is a Lieb-type trace concavity theorem [Lieb73; Ando79; Epstein73].
   In the library, that core was already formalized (for the sandwiched DPI), which is
   why this route closes the open `sorry` with no new deep analysis: the contribution
   is the limit argument, the $[0,\infty]$-valued case analysis, and the kernel/support
   bookkeeping.

## 4. Notes on citations inside the Lean source

Two pre-existing comments in `DPI.lean` (not introduced by this contribution) deserve a
follow-up fix, flagged here for transparency:

- The module header (`DPI.lean:36`) says "Following Leditzky–Rouzé–Datta
  (arXiv:1306.5920)". The name/eprint pairing is off: **arXiv:1306.5920 is Beigi**
  [Beigi13]; Leditzky–Rouzé–Datta is **arXiv:1604.02119** [LRD17]. Both eprint pages
  were checked directly on arXiv (2026-07-06) while preparing this note, so this is
  confirmed, not suspected. The *approach* actually followed (variational formula,
  twirling) is indeed Frank–Lieb as presented by Leditzky–Rouzé–Datta, so the names are
  right and the eprint number is wrong. We are happy to submit the one-token fix
  (`1306.5920` → `1604.02119`) as a trivial follow-up if the maintainer would like it;
  it is deliberately *not* bundled into this PR to keep the PR single-concept.
- The docstring of `sandwichedTraceFunctional_jointly_convex` says "This is Proposition
  3 of the paper, originally from Frank–Lieb". This refers to the Leditzky–Rouzé–Datta
  presentation; the proposition number has not been re-verified against [LRD17] while
  preparing this note.

## 5. References

Entries marked **[verified]** were checked against their arXiv abstract pages (and,
where noted, the publisher's listing) on 2026-07-06: title, full author list, eprint
number, and the journal reference/DOI as shown there. Entries marked **[memory]** are
standard references transcribed from memory: authors, titles and venues are stated with
confidence, but volume/page/year details should be double-checked before this note is
published anywhere citable. (Most [memory] entries predate arXiv, so no eprint check is
possible; none is load-bearing for the correctness claims in this packet.)

- **[Umegaki62]** [memory] H. Umegaki, *Conditional expectation in an operator algebra,
  IV (entropy and information)*, Kōdai Math. Sem. Rep. **14** (1962), 59–85.
- **[Lieb73]** [memory] E. H. Lieb, *Convex trace functions and the
  Wigner–Yanase–Dyson conjecture*, Adv. Math. **11** (1973), 267–288.
- **[LiebRuskai73]** [memory] E. H. Lieb, M. B. Ruskai, *Proof of the strong
  subadditivity of quantum-mechanical entropy* (with an appendix by B. Simon),
  J. Math. Phys. **14** (1973), 1938–1941.
- **[LiebRuskaiPRL73]** [memory] E. H. Lieb, M. B. Ruskai, *A fundamental property of
  quantum-mechanical entropy*, Phys. Rev. Lett. **30** (1973), 434–436.
- **[Epstein73]** [memory] H. Epstein, *Remarks on two theorems of E. Lieb*,
  Commun. Math. Phys. **31** (1973), 317–325.
- **[Lindblad74]** [memory] G. Lindblad, *Expectations and entropy inequalities for
  finite quantum systems*, Commun. Math. Phys. **39** (1974), 111–119.
  (Joint convexity of the relative entropy.)
- **[Lindblad75]** [memory] G. Lindblad, *Completely positive maps and entropy
  inequalities*, Commun. Math. Phys. **40** (1975), 147–151. (Monotonicity under CP
  maps.)
- **[Uhlmann77]** [memory] A. Uhlmann, *Relative entropy and the
  Wigner–Yanase–Dyson–Lieb concavity in an interpolation theory*, Commun. Math. Phys.
  **54** (1977), 21–32.
- **[Ando79]** [memory] T. Ando, *Concavity of certain maps on positive definite
  matrices and applications to Hadamard products*, Linear Algebra Appl. **26** (1979),
  203–241.
- **[Petz86]** [memory] D. Petz, *Quasi-entropies for finite quantum systems*,
  Rep. Math. Phys. **23** (1986), 57–65.
- **[Effros09]** [memory] E. G. Effros, *A matrix convexity approach to some celebrated
  quantum inequalities*, Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. USA **106** (2009), 1006–1008.
- **[Carlen10]** [memory] E. A. Carlen, *Trace inequalities and quantum entropy: an
  introductory course*, in Entropy and the Quantum, Contemp. Math. **529**, Amer. Math.
  Soc. (2010), 73–140. (The Lean source's comment "Theorem 2.10 of Carlen" for trace
  function convexity refers to these notes.)
- **[Tropp12]** [memory] J. A. Tropp, *From joint convexity of quantum relative entropy
  to a concavity theorem of Lieb*, Proc. Amer. Math. Soc. **140** (2012), 1757–1760.
- **[MLDSFT13]** [verified] M. Müller-Lennert, F. Dupuis, O. Szehr, S. Fehr,
  M. Tomamichel, *On quantum Rényi entropies: a new generalization and some properties*,
  J. Math. Phys. **54**, 122203 (2013). arXiv:1306.3142,
  doi:10.1063/1.4838856.
- **[WWY14]** [verified] M. M. Wilde, A. Winter, D. Yang, *Strong converse for the
  classical capacity of entanglement-breaking and Hadamard channels via a sandwiched
  Rényi relative entropy*, Commun. Math. Phys. **331** (2014), 593–622.
  arXiv:1306.1586, doi:10.1007/s00220-014-2122-x.
- **[FrankLieb13]** [verified] R. L. Frank, E. H. Lieb, *Monotonicity of a relative
  Rényi entropy*, J. Math. Phys. **54**, 122201 (2013). arXiv:1306.5358,
  doi:10.1063/1.4838835. (Article number 122201 confirmed against the AIP listing.)
- **[Beigi13]** [verified] S. Beigi, *Sandwiched Rényi divergence satisfies data
  processing inequality*, J. Math. Phys. **54**, 122202 (2013). arXiv:1306.5920,
  doi:10.1063/1.4838855. (Article number confirmed against the arXiv journal
  reference.)
- **[LRD17]** [verified] F. Leditzky, C. Rouzé, N. Datta, *Data processing for the
  sandwiched Rényi divergence: a condition for equality*, Lett. Math. Phys. **107**
  (2017), 61–80. arXiv:1604.02119, doi:10.1007/s11005-016-0896-9.
- **[Hiai13]** [memory] F. Hiai, *Concavity of certain matrix trace and norm
  functions*, Linear Algebra Appl. **439** (2013), 1568–1589. (arXiv id not verified.)
- **[MosonyiOgawa15]** [verified] M. Mosonyi, T. Ogawa, *Quantum hypothesis testing and
  the operational interpretation of the quantum Rényi relative entropies*,
  Commun. Math. Phys. **334** (2015), 1617–1648. arXiv:1309.3228,
  doi:10.1007/s00220-014-2248-x.
- **[Tomamichel16]** [verified] M. Tomamichel, *Quantum Information Processing with
  Finite Resources: Mathematical Foundations*, SpringerBriefs in Mathematical Physics
  vol. 5, Springer (2016). arXiv:1504.00233, doi:10.1007/978-3-319-21891-5.
  (Series volume 5 confirmed against the publisher listing.)
- **[Wilde17]** [memory] M. M. Wilde, *Quantum Information Theory*, 2nd ed., Cambridge
  University Press (2017).
- **[Spohn78]** [memory] H. Spohn, *Entropy production for quantum dynamical
  semigroups*, J. Math. Phys. **19** (1978), 1227–1230. (Cited in the physics brief for
  the second-law connection; page range from memory.)
